page: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume and runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile. The instance achieves strong results in single-threaded tasks, including Geekbench single-core testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, alongside top-tier performance in Geekbench text processing. However, multi-core performance is comparatively limited, with weak ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, while small-block cached read-only bandwidth is weak.

The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in localized vector and matrix operations. In LLM inference testing, the server demonstrates average performance for prompt processing and text generation using small 135M models, but performance drops to weak or poor levels when handling medium 7B models. Database operations also rank as weak, indicating that while the local NVMe SSD provides fast local storage, the instance is not optimized for heavy multi-threaded database workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and fast local NVMe storage without the premium of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, light web serving, and software compilation tasks that benefit from high single-core clock speeds and local temporary storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 2 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, though multi-core performance and database operations are relatively weak. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, enabling efficient vector operations. Qualitatively, this instance provides a balanced, cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated compute and fast local storage. It is well-suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, light web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD. It delivers strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB local NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A general-purpose instance combining strong single-core performance and high-speed DDR5 memory with local NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 237 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression tasks
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Best suited for single-threaded applications, light web serving, and code compilation
